summary: DeVault argues that custom JavaScript form controls destroy the rich, nuanced behaviors that users depend on from native browser inputs. He catalogs the many subtle interaction patterns built into standard text boxes and select elements on Linux/Firefox, including word-wise selection, primary selection clipboard, and keyboard-driven list navigation. He points out that these behaviors vary across operating systems, browsers, languages, and accessibility needs, making it impossible for any custom implementation to replicate them all. The post concludes that JavaScript-powered form replacements make forms slower for sighted users and completely broken for blind users.
key_points: Native browser form controls encode hundreds of subtle interaction behaviors that users internalize into muscle memory over years of use
Custom JavaScript form controls fail to replicate platform-specific nuances like X11 primary selection, word-wise selection expansion, and keyboard-driven list navigation
Accessibility is devastated by custom controls: blind users who rely on standardized behavior are left stranded by bespoke implementations
The exact interaction nuances vary across operating systems, browsers, natural languages, hardware, and physical ability, making faithful reimplementation effectively impossible
Tasks that take under a second with native controls can balloon to a minute with custom replacements
money_quotes: You cannot capture all of this nuance in a home-grown form control, or even anything close to it, but many JavaScript programmers do it anyway.
Whenever I encounter a custom form control, the time required to complete the form increases from under a second to as much as a minute.
When you hit a JavaScript-powered organic cage-free non-GMO text box, you're screwed.
There are dozens of tiny workflows that people depend on every day that have never even occurred to you.
Making a custom form control with JavaScript is going to make life worse for a lot of people. Just don't do it.
The browser's built-in controls are quite sufficient.
There are hundreds of little nuances that users learn to use their computers efficiently.
key_insight: Native browser form controls encode an impossibly deep stack of platform-specific, language-specific, and accessibility-critical behaviors that no custom JavaScript replacement can faithfully reproduce.
